items.hook: Swedish–Novgorodian Wars: A Novgorodian army led by Alexander Nevsky defeats the Swedes in the Battle of the Neva.
items.body: The Swedish–Novgorodian Wars were a series of armed conflicts in the 12th and 13th centuries between the Novgorod Republic and medieval Sweden over control of the Gulf of Finland. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Founded in 2003 as a nonprofit organization, the museum exhibits historic and contemporary ceramic artwork from both its permanent collection of 10,000 objects and through temporary rotating exhibitions.
items.body: The American Museum of Ceramic Art (AMOCA) is an art museum for ceramic art, located in Pomona, California. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Spanish Inquisition is officially disbanded after nearly 356 years.
items.body: The Tribunal of the Holy Office of the Inquisition was authorized by Pope Sixtus IV in 1478 and the first inquisitors, Miguel de Morillo and Juan de San Martín, were appointed by the future Catholic Monarchs, King Ferdinand II of Aragon and Queen Isabella I of Castile, in 1480. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Aleksei Chirikov sights land in Southeast Alaska. He sends men ashore in a longboat, making them the first Europeans to visit Alaska.
items.body: Aleksei Ilyich Chirikov was a Russian navigator and captain who, along with Vitus Bering, was the first Russian to reach the northwest coast of North America. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Raza de Bronce is a 1919 novel by the Bolivian writer Alcides Arguedas.
items.body: It is considered one of the most important novels in Bolivian literature, and has been recognized as such by a number of critical studies as well as by the Bolivian government. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Factions of the Turkish Armed Forces attempt a coup.
items.body: The Turkish Armed Forces are the military forces of the Republic of Turkey. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Daniel Isn't Real is a 2019 American psychological horror film directed by Adam Egypt Mortimer, from a screenplay by Mortimer and Brian DeLeeuw, based upon the novel In This Way I Was Saved by DeLeeuw.
items.body: It stars Miles Robbins, Patrick Schwarzenegger, Sasha Lane, Mary Stuart Masterson, Hannah Marks, Chukwudi Iwuji and Peter McRobbie. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Daikaiju Deburasu is a 1990 strategy video game for the Family Computer.
items.body: The game involves has a kaiju-movie theme, where the player assists the military in guiding a giant egg through a large city and then protecting it from a huge monster that rises from the sea. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Ichigensan - The Newcomer is Swiss author David Zoppetti's debut novel.
items.body: Written in Japanese, in 1996 it won the 20th Subaru Prize, awarded to new works by Subaru Novel Magazine, and was published by Shueisha that year. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Tomek in the Land of Kangaroos is a youth adventure novel by Alfred Szklarski, in 1957.
items.body: It tells about the hunting expedition into Australia of Tomek Wilmowski and his father. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Soul Music is a fantasy novel by British writer Terry Pratchett, the sixteenth book in the Discworld series, first published in 1994.
items.body: Like many of Pratchett's novels it introduces an element of modern society into the magical and vaguely late medieval, early modern world of the Discworld, in this case Rock and Roll music and stardom, with near disastrous consequences. One of those things you

items.hook: In his book Clinical Psychiatry, Emil Kraepelin gives a name to Alzheimer's disease, naming it after his colleague Alois Alzheimer.
items.body: Emil Wilhelm Georg Magnus Kraepelin was a German psychiatrist. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.hook: Napoleonic Wars: Napoleon Bonaparte surrenders aboard HMS Bellerophon.
items.body: The Napoleonic Wars (1803–15) were a global series of conflicts fought by a fluctuating array of European coalitions against the French First Republic (1803–04) under the First Consul followed by the First French Empire (1804–15) under the Emperor of the French, Napoleon. The world is stranger than it lets on.
